This layer is also referred to as the subcutaneous tissue or adipose layer [9]. The injection delivers medication to the tissues between the skin and the muscle [1]. Common sites for administration include the fat tissues behind the arm [1], the abdomen or thigh [7], the lower abdomen, lower back, buttocks, or hips [8].

question: when did they replace lead with graphite in pencils

question: all the motor neurons that control the skeletal muscles are
model_answer: All the motor neurons that control the skeletal muscles are lower motor neurons [19].
